title: Psychotria fleuryana
intro: Psychotria fleuryana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the 'Yellow Bristle' or 'Yellow Buttonbush'. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria fleuryana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been associated with its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Psychotria fleuryana is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Psychotria fleuryana is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises over 13,000 species, including many plants with medicinal, ornamental, and food value. The genus Psychotria is a large group with approximately 1,800 species, many of which are found in tropical regions. Psychotria fleuryana is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, where it grows as a shrub or small tree. Its classification within the Rubiaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this diverse and ecologically important group.
